M-Scale Bid Cap



Use this alongside the training video to implement this strategy.



Should you run bid cap? This is my preference for manual bid scaling however, I usually turn to manual bid when speed of scaling is most important or if I feel Facebook is holding me back due to poor performance in my funnel out of my control. Bid cap bidding is the most reliable as I can control the auction bid, and works best with a proven audience, ad, funnel and offer. Without this, you could end up wasting away a lot of budget.



Duplicate your best audience into a separate CBO manual bid test campaign (illustrated below) - set the BID CAP for as 1X, 2X, 3X, 4X your CPA target as bid caps, duplicating the same ad set in your new CBO. I go wide on bid levels then narrow down. Some prefer to start with one bid, perhaps lower, then work up - so for example, run a single ad set with a bid of say 1X your CPA target, then gradually increase it - speed of results matters to me so I find this method I use faster.



Total CBO budget = (BID 1 x 5) + (BID 2 x 5) + (BID 3 x 5) etc.

If your bid cap is $50 as per the illustration above, your total budget = (50 x 5) + (100 x 5) + (150 x 5) + (200 x 5) = $2500

What we’re doing here is ensuring the ad sets are given enough spending power to test the bid caps

You’re NOT planning to spend $2500 if results are not where they need to be

Set a MIN budget at the ad set level so that each gets enough of a push initially - you can optionally set a MAX to ensure, to an extent, during the early phase one ad set doesn’t run off with all the spend too early:

MIN budget = CPA x 2

Optional: MAX budget = CPA x 5

Remove this after day 3

Run normal ads, not DCO ads as DCO generally works better once the ad set is stable

Run 2-4 ads per ad set. Ideally if you have 2 really strong ads, use those duplicated once (so AD 1, AD1-copy, AD2, AD2-copy using shared Post IDS in the copies)

Ads: Use rules (automated or manual checks) at the AD level as below:

LOWER RISK: Pause ads if:

Spend at ad level is > 2X your CPA target with no conversions

Spend at ad level is > 4X your CPA and CPA over 50% of your target

Spend at ad level is > 6X your CPA and CPA higher than 25% of your target

HIGHER RISK (though giving more data and spend to increase chance of finding a positive audience pocket): Pause ads if:

Spend at ad level is > 4X your CPA with no conversions

Spend at ad level is > 8X your CPA and CPA over 50% of your target

Spend at ad level is > 12X your CPA and CPA higher than 25% of your target

Ad sets: Use rules (automated or manual checks) at the AD SET level as below:

LOWER RISK: Pause the ad set if:

Spend at ad set level is > 5X your CPA with no conversions

Spend at ad set level is > 5X your CPA and CPA over 25% of your target

Spend at ad set level is > 10X your CPA and CPA higher than your target

HIGHER RISK: Don’t pause the ad sets, let the ad level rules kick-in only. This allows for maximum learning but relies on ad level rules performing strongly enough

We track rules manually until we’re confident on performance before building the rules into software

Cycle the ads (pausing if they’re bad, restarting the next day) over 3 days (or 3 cycles) until performance settles - it should be quickly obvious which ads are performing best in which ad set - it’s usually between 2X and 3X bids that work well on average. If you have a single winner, use that on your other audiences by pushing them into a new CBO with manual bid.

Split your ad sets by age, gender, country, region (as relevant) to see if bids differ across those primarily. Then also see if bids differ by placement and device.
